kansas-city.neighborhoods: Downtown, Westport, Country Club Plaza, Crossroads Arts District, River Market, Brookside
kansas-city.meetingHighlights: Large metro fellowship with cross-state meetings, many speaker nights, and a robust schedule of lunchtime and evening groups.
kansas-city.transitInfo: RideKC bus network and regional transit connect the metro; Kansas City International Airport (MCI) serves national and international flights.

springfield.neighborhoods: Downtown, Midtown, Galloway, Rountree, South Campbell, Northern Heights
springfield.meetingHighlights: Active downtown and suburban meetings with several AA service groups and regular speaker events.
springfield.transitInfo: Springfield Transit Services provides local routes; Springfield–Branson National Airport for regional flights.

st-louis.neighborhoods: Downtown, The Delmar Loop, Central West End, Soulard, Lafayette Square, Clayton
st-louis.meetingHighlights: Extensive meeting options across neighborhoods and suburbs with long-established groups and numerous specialty meetings.
st-louis.transitInfo: MetroLink light rail and MetroBus provide transit across the region; St. Louis Lambert International Airport (STL) serves national flights.

columbia.meetingHighlights: University-area meetings with strong student involvement and a mix of lunchtime, evening, and study-format meetings.
columbia.transitInfo: Go COMO provides local transit; Columbia Regional Airport serves regional flights.

jefferson-city.meetingHighlights: Capital-city meetings with steady weekday attendance and several civic-group hosted meetings.
jefferson-city.transitInfo: Jefferson City Transit provides local service; regional highways and small regional airports connect the area.

independence.neighborhoods: Downtown Independence, Noland Road, Blue Ridge, Santa Fe Hills
independence.meetingHighlights: Active suburban meetings serving eastern KC metro; strong Truman Library area fellowship with regular speaker meetings.
independence.transitInfo: RideKC buses connect Independence to Kansas City metro; Kansas City International Airport (MCI) accessible via highway.

joplin.areaDescription: Joplin and the Four State area in southwest Missouri
joplin.neighborhoods: Downtown Joplin, Murphysburg, Eastmoreland, North Heights, West Joplin, Carl Junction
joplin.meetingHighlights: Four State recovery community rebuilt after the 2011 tornado; resilient fellowship with meetings in downtown and rebuilt community centers; serves southwest Missouri, southeast Kansas, and northeast Oklahoma.
joplin.transitInfo: Joplin Globe Express trolley and dial-a-ride service; Joplin Regional Airport (JLN); I-44 and US-71 provide regional access.

cape-girardeau.areaDescription: Cape Girardeau and the southeast Missouri river region
cape-girardeau.neighborhoods: Downtown Cape, SEMO campus area, West Park, North Cape, Jackson (nearby)
cape-girardeau.meetingHighlights: Mississippi River town recovery with meetings near Southeast Missouri State University and downtown; cross-river fellowship with southern Illinois; evening discussion and Big Book study groups.
cape-girardeau.transitInfo: Cape Girardeau Transit provides local bus service; Cape Girardeau Regional Airport (CGI); I-55 corridor along the Mississippi River.

st-charles.areaDescription: Historic Missouri River city in the western St. Louis metro
st-charles.neighborhoods: Historic Main Street, Frenchtown, New Town, St. Charles Convention Center area, Zumbehl corridor, Duchesne
st-charles.meetingHighlights: Meetings serve St. Charles County with strong access from western St. Louis suburbs.
st-charles.transitInfo: MetroBus regional links and nearby I-70, I-64, and Lambert International Airport support travel.

lees-summit.areaDescription: Large eastern suburb in the Kansas City metropolitan area
lees-summit.neighborhoods: Downtown, Raintree Lake area, Lakewood, New Longview, Summit Fair district, Pryor Road corridor
lees-summit.meetingHighlights: Meetings serve eastern Jackson County with dependable suburban access and regular evening options.
lees-summit.transitInfo: Primarily car-oriented via US-50, I-470, and MO-291; Kansas City International Airport is the nearest major airport.

branson.areaDescription: Entertainment and tourism center in the Ozarks
branson.neighborhoods: Downtown, Branson Landing, Theatre District, Hollister vicinity, Table Rock Lake area, Gretna Road corridor
branson.meetingHighlights: Meetings serve local residents, hospitality workers, and visitors across the Branson area.
branson.transitInfo: Road access via US-65 and MO-76; Branson Airport and nearby Springfield-Branson National Airport support travel.
